concerns.text: Task T032 mandates using the Lincoln-Petersen Capture-Recapture estimator for precision/recall. This contradicts FR-006 and SC-001 which require measuring against an expert-validated ground truth baseline (TP/FP/FN counts) derived from PR comments. Capture-Recapture is a population estimation technique, not a direct precision/recall calculation against a known baseline, effectively weakening the requirement to measure actual tool performance.

Task T036 implements Bonferroni correction. The Plan (Section: Computational Feasibility & Statistical Rigor) explicitly rejects Bonferroni as 'too conservative' and mandates a 'max-t permutation procedure' for FWER control. This task silently relaxes the statistical rigor required by the plan.

Task T034 specifies a 'Fixed-effects regression' model. The Spec (FR-008) and Plan (Complexity Tracking) explicitly require 'Mixed-effects regression' (LMM) to handle project-level clustering and avoid overfitting with small cluster sizes. Using fixed-effects here violates the methodological constraint for handling hierarchical data.

Task T028 mandates implementing a ±5 line tolerance window for sensitivity analysis. The Spec (FR-005) and Plan restrict this tolerance to a fallback mechanism 'if AST unavailable', not as a primary sensitivity parameter. This task elevates a fallback to a primary feature, violating the alignment methodology constraints.

Task T039 includes a post-hoc power analysis check. The Plan (Assumptions) treats the 30–40 repository sample size as a fixed constraint with mixed-effects models as the mitigation. A post-hoc power check on a fixed, small N is methodologically contradictory to the design which assumes the sample size is adequate for the chosen model, potentially introducing a 'stopping rule' bias not present in the plan.

Task T027 description states: 'Primary method is AST-based diff matching; IF AST unavailable, fallback to ±5 line tolerance'. While this aligns with FR-005, the task description conflates 'AST-based' with 'diff matching'. FR-005 distinguishes 'AST-based or diff-based semantic alignment'. The task should clarify that the primary method is AST-based, and diff-based (or line tolerance) is the fallback, ensuring the semantic distinction in the spec is preserved.
